faqs.question: What are you up to at Stanford?
faqs.answer: I help organize TreeHacks, the world's largest collegiate hackathon where thousands of students create innovative projects in just 36 hours. I'm also part of ASES (the entrepreneurial society) and play on the badminton club team.

faqs.question: What are you building right now?
faqs.answer: I'm developing an iOS wellness app that integrates AI into daily health routines to help people with their overall wellness. It's exciting because it combines everything I care about—technology, human connection, and actually helping people access support.

faqs.question: What research have you done?
faqs.answer: In high school, I did particle physics research at Fermilab studying Dark Photons and the Higgs Boson. At Stanford, I switched to cybersecurity and built a ZDNS validation tool for DNSSEC to make internet infrastructure more secure.

faqs.question: How can I best reach you?
faqs.answer: The best way to reach me is through email at dbarrow@stanford.edu. I love talking to people about projects, ideas, or really anything interesting. Always happy for a good conversation.

faqs.question: What subjects are you most interested in?
faqs.answer: The sweet spot where computer science, math, and building real products intersect. I love the theoretical side of CS, but what really drives me is using that knowledge to create something people actually want to use.

faqs.question: What do you like to do in your free time?
faqs.answer: I play soccer, tennis, and badminton with friends, teach violin lessons, and like building iOS apps in my spare time. Always working on some kind of project.
